commands.review_summary:
The PR carries fenced-code language metadata through the shared Markdown IR and renders Telegram code blocks as `<pre><code class="language-...">`, with a formatter regression test.

Reproducibility: yes. source inspection gives a high-confidence current-main repro: Telegram uses a static `<pre><code>` opener and the Markdown IR currently drops fence `info`, so a ```bash fence cannot produce `language-bash`. I did not run Vitest because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: Needs contributor/live Telegram proof and maintainer acknowledgement of the additive SDK renderer type before merge; there is no narrow code defect for ClawSweeper to repair.

target.title: fix(telegram): preserve fenced code languages
target.body:
Summary - Preserve fenced code block language info in the shared Markdown IR. - Allow renderers to build style openings from span metadata. - Emit Telegram fenced code blocks as `
` when a fence language is present.

Tests
- RED: `node --no-maglev node_modules/vitest/vitest.mjs run extensions/telegram/src/format.test.ts --reporter=verbose` failed before the implementation: expected `class="language-bash"`, received plain `
`.
- GREEN: `node --no-maglev node_modules/vitest/vitest.mjs run extensions/telegram/src/format.test.ts --reporter=verbose` passed (29 tests).
- `node node_modules/oxfmt/bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/markdown/ir.ts src/markdown/render.ts src/markdown/render-aware-chunking.ts extensions/telegram/src/format.ts extensions/telegram/src/format.test.ts` passed.
- `node node_modules/oxlint/bin/oxlint --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json src/markdown/ir.ts src/markdown/render.ts src/markdown/render-aware-chunking.ts extensions/telegram/src/format.ts extensions/telegram/src/format.test.ts` passed.
- `node_modules/@typescript/native-preview-darwin-arm64/lib/tsgo -p tsconfig.core.json --noEmit && node_modules/@typescript/native-preview-darwin-arm64/lib/tsgo -p tsconfig.extensions.json --noEmit` passed.
- `git diff --check` passed.

Behavior addressed: Telegram markdown rendering now preserves a fenced code language such as `bash` in the Bot API-supported `
` form.
Real environment tested: macOS local checkout, targeted Telegram formatter test and core/extension type checks.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node --no-maglev node_modules/vitest/vitest.mjs run extensions/telegram/src/format.test.ts --reporter=verbose`.
Evidence after fix: The new regression test for a bash fenced code block passed along with the existing Telegram formatter suite.
Observed result after fix: `markdownToTelegramHtml("```bash\necho \"hello\"\n```")` renders `
echo "hello"\n
`.
What was not tested: Live Telegram Bot API delivery was not exercised.

Fixes #10519
target.author: leno23
